databaseSqlOverlay.reason: database/sql itself compiles successfully with TinyGo wasip2 (confirmed in tinygo-stdlib.json). The blocking issues are in the driver libraries' crypto/tls dependencies, not in database/sql. The WarpGrid database proxy shim (US-112) handles connections at the host level, bypassing driver compilation entirely.

drivers.name: go-sql-driver/mysql
drivers.goTestDetails: All 5 tests pass: sql.Open, Ping error path, SELECT 1 query construction, CRUD sequence, MySQL placeholder syntax, driver registration, type imports

errors.description: TinyGo's crypto/tls.Config does not implement Clone() method
TinyGo does not implement tls.X509KeyPair for certificate parsing

blockingStdlibDeps.notes: go-sql-driver/mysql uses TLS config cloning and certificate loading for secure connections

workarounds.description: Use WarpGrid's host-side database proxy shim (US-112) which manages TCP connections and TLS termination at the Wasmtime host level. Guest code sends/receives raw MySQL wire protocol bytes through the proxy.

featuresTested.notes: Driver registration and sql.Open work in standard Go
DDL execution via db.Exec with AUTO_INCREMENT

drivers.name: go-redis/redis
drivers.goTestDetails: All 5 tests pass: NewClient, Ping error path, Options configuration, PING/SET/GET command construction, type imports

errors.description: TinyGo's crypto/tls.Config does not implement Clone() method
TinyGo does not implement net.Resolver type

blockingStdlibDeps.notes: go-redis uses TLS config cloning for secure connections
go-redis optionally uses net.Resolver for custom DNS; not required for basic operation

workarounds.description: Use WarpGrid's host-side database proxy shim (US-112) which manages TCP connections and TLS termination at the Wasmtime host level. Guest code sends/receives raw Redis RESP protocol bytes through the proxy.

featuresTested.notes: Client creation with redis.Options works in standard Go
